transparent}#wm-ipp{z-index:2147483647}#wm-ipp,#wm-ipp *{font-family:Lucida Grande,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;font-size:12px;line-height:1.2;letter-spacing:0;width:auto;height:auto;max-width:none;max-height:none;min-width:0!important;min-height:0;outline:none;float:none;text-align:left;border:none;color:#000;text-indent:0;position:initial;background:none}#wm-ipp div,#wm-ipp canvas{display:block}#wm-ipp div,#wm-ipp tr,#wm-ipp td,#wm-ipp a,#wm-ipp form{padding:0;margin:0;border:none;border-radius:0;background-color:transparent;background-image:none;height:auto}#wm-ipp table{border:none;border-collapse:collapse;margin:0;padding:0;width:auto;font-size:inherit}#wm-ipp form input{padding:1px!important;height:auto;display:inline;margin:0;color:#000;background:none #fff;border:1px solid #666}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]{padding:0 8px!important;margin:1px 0 1px 5px!important;width:auto!important;border:1px solid #000!important;background:#fff!important;color:#000!important}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]:hover{background:#eee!important;cursor:pointer!important}#wm-ipp form input[type=submit]:active{transform:translateY(1px)}#wm-ipp a{display:inline}#wm-ipp a:hover{text-decoration:underline}#wm-ipp a.wm-btn:hover{text-decoration:none;color:#ff0!important}#wm-ipp a.wm-btn:hover span{color:#ff0!important}#wm-ipp #wm-ipp-inside{margin:0 6px;border:5px solid #000;border-top:none;background-color:rgba(255,255,255,0.9);-moz-box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;-webkit-box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;box-shadow:1px 1px 4px #333;border-radius:0 0 8px 8px}#wm-ipp #wm-logo{padding:0 10px;vertical-align:middle;flex:0 0 100px}#wm-ipp .c{padding-left:4px}#wm-ipp .c .u{margin-top:4px!important}#wm-ipp .n{padding:0 0 0 5px!important;vertical-align:bottom}#wm-ipp .n a{text-decoration:none;color:#33f;font-weight:bold}#wm-ipp .n .b{padding:0 6px 0 0!important;text-align:right!important;overflow:visible;white-space:nowrap;color:#99a;vertical-align:middle}#wm-ipp .n .y .b{padding:0 6px 2px 0!important}#wm-ipp .n .c{background:#000;color:#ff0;font-weight:bold;padding:0!important;text-align:center}#wm-ipp .n .d span.ta{display:inline-block;width:0;height:0;border-style:solid}#wm-ipp .n .d .b span.ta{border-width:8px 14px 8px 0}#wm-ipp .n .d .f span.ta{border-width:8px 0 8px 14px}#wm-ipp .n .d a span.ta{border-color:transparent #33f}#wm-ipp .n .d a span.ta:hover{border-color:transparent #77f}#wm-ipp .n td.f{padding:0 0 0 6px!important;text-align:left!important;overflow:visible;white-space:nowrap;color:#99a;vertical-align:middle}#wm-ipp .n tr.m td{text-transform:uppercase;white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .s{vertical-align:bottom}#wm-ipp #wm-nav-captures{white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .s a.t{color:#33f;font-weight:bold;line-height:1.8}#wm-ipp .c .s div.r{color:#666;font-size:9px;white-space:nowrap}#wm-ipp .c .k{padding-bottom:1px}#wm-ipp .c .s{padding:0 5px 2px 0!important}#wm-ipp td#displayMonthEl{padding:2px 0!important}#wm-ipp td#displayYearEl{padding:0 0 2px 0!important}div#wm-ipp-sparkline{white-space:nowrap;background-color:#fff;cursor:pointer;line-height:0.9}#wm-sparkline-canvas{position:relative;z-index:9012;max-width:none}#wm-ipp-sparkline div.yt{position:absolute;z-index:9010!important;background-color:#ff0!important;top:0}#wm-ipp-sparkline div.mt{position:absolute;z-index:9013!important;background-color:#ec008c!important;top:0}#wm-ipp .r{margin-left:4px}#wm-ipp .r a{color:#33f;border:none;position:relative;background-color:transparent;background-repeat:no-repeat!important;background-position:100% 100%!important;text-decoration:none}#wm-ipp #wm-capinfo{border-radius:0 0 4px 4px}#wm-ipp #wm-capinfo a.wm-selector:hover{color:#fff}#wm-ipp #wm-expand{right:1px;bottom:-1px;color:#ffffff;background-color:#666!important;padding:0 5px 0 3px!important;border-radius:3px 3px 0 0!important}#wm-ipp #wm-expand span{color:#ffffff}#wm-ipp #wm-expand #wm-expand-icon{display:inline-block;transition:transform 0.5s;transform-origin:50% 45%}#wm-ipp #wmtb{text-align:right}#wm-ipp #wmtb #wmtbURL{width:calc(100% - 45px)}#wm-ipp 4\. Getting around in Felix — Felix Manual 1.7.1.1 documentation

#86989B;font-weight:bold;background-color:#AFC1C4}dt:target{background-color:#fbe54e}.highlight-python{background-color:#cfc;padding:8px;border:1px solid #8f8;margin-bottom:5px}div.header{padding-top:10px;padding-bottom:10px}div.header .headertitle{font-family:"Georgia","Times New Roman",serif;font-weight:normal;font-size:180%;letter-spacing:.08em;margin-bottom:.8em}div.header .headertitle a{color:white}div.header div.rel{margin-top:1em}div.header div.rel a{color:#fcaf3e;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase}div.content-wrapper{background-color:white;padding-top:20px;padding-bottom:20px}div.document{width:50em;float:left}div.body{padding-right:2em;text-align:justify}div.document h1{line-height:120%}div.document ul{margin:1.5em;list-style-type:square}div.document dd{margin-left:1.2em;margin-top:.4em;margin-bottom:1em}div.document .section{margin-top:1.7em}div.document .section:first-child{margin-top:0px}div.document h2{margin-top:.7em}div.document p{margin-bottom:.5em}div.document li.toctree-l1{margin-bottom:1em}div.document .docutils.literal{background-color:#eeeeec;padding:1px}div.document blockquote{margin:1em}div.sidebar{width:20em;float:right;font-size:.9em}div.sidebar a,div.header a{text-decoration:none}div.sidebar a:hover,div.header a:hover{text-decoration:underline}div.sidebar h3{color:#2e3436;text-transform:uppercase;font-size:130%;letter-spacing:.1em}div.sidebar ul{list-style-type:none}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1 a{display:block;padding:1px;border:1px solid #dddddd;background-color:#eeeeec;margin-bottom:.4em;padding-left:3px;color:#2e3436}div.sidebar li.toctree-l2 a{background-color:transparent;border:none;margin-left:1em;border-bottom:1px solid #dddddd}div.sidebar li.toctree-l2:last-child a{border-bottom:none}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1.current a{border-right:5px solid #fcaf3e}div.sidebar li.toctree-l1.current li.toctree-l2 a{border-right:none}div.sidebar input[type="text"]{width:170px}div.sidebar input[type="submit"]{width:30px}div.footer-wrapper{background:url(data:,)top left repeat-x;border-top:4px solid #babdb6;padding-top:10px;padding-bottom:10px;min-height:80px}div.footer,div.footer a{color:#888a85}div.footer .right{text-align:right}div.footer .left{text-transform:uppercase}.figure{margin-top:1em;margin-bottom:1em}.caption{margin-top:.5em;margin-left:1em;font-style:italic}div.viewcode-block:target{margin:-1px -3px;padding:0 Using Felix for the First Time">previous</a> \| <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/add-entry.html" accesskey="N" title="4.2.1. Add an Entry">next</a> \| <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/genindex.html" accesskey="I" title="General Index">index</a>

<div class="content-wrapper">

<div class="bodywrapper">

<div class="body">

<div id="getting-around-in-felix" class="section">

<span id="getting-around"></span>

# 4. Getting around in Felix<a href="#getting-around-in-felix" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

<div id="getting-started" class="section">

## 4.1. Getting Started<a href="#getting-started" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

To launch Felix, double click on the **Felix** icon. Felix will also launch automatically if you invoke it from one of the Microsoft Office interfaces or from TagAssist.

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

When you start Felix, it remembers the last position and window size, as well as the GUI language that you used. This is handy when you adjust your memory and glossary windows to be just right for your screen and office program setup.

</div>

</div>

<div id="the-memory-window" class="section">

## 4.2. The Memory Window<a href="#the-memory-window" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

![Felix Memory window](data:image/png;base64,)

The Felix memory window displays the segments (sentences) you look up in your translation memories (TMs), as well as any suggested translations Felix may find.

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

You can zoom the size of the text in the memory window in and out. Click on the title bar of the window to make sure it’s active, then use Ctrl + Mouse Wheel to make the text larger or smaller.

</div>

This section describes the various features of the memory window, as well as the menu and toolbar items.

**Sub-sections:**

<div class="toctree-wrapper compound">

- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/add-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.2.1. Add an Entry</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/matches.html" class="reference internal">4.2.2. Translation Matches</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/edit-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.2.3. Edit an Entry</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/quick-search.html" class="reference internal">4.2.4. Quick Search</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/delete-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.2.5. Delete an Entry</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/register-gloss-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.2.6. Add Glossary Entries from a Translation</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/open-memory.html" class="reference internal">4.2.7. Open a Memory</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/save-memory.html" class="reference internal">4.2.8. Save the Memory</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/create-memory.html" class="reference internal">4.2.9. Create a New Memory</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/change-language.html" class="reference internal">4.2.10. Change the UI Language</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/gloss-win.html" class="reference internal">4.2.11. Open a New Glossary Window</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html" class="reference internal">4.2.12. Set User Preferences</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html#general-page" class="reference internal">4.2.12.1. General Page</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html#view-page" class="reference internal">4.2.12.2. View Page</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html#memory-page" class="reference internal">4.2.12.3. Memory Page</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html#glossary-page" class="reference internal">4.2.12.4. Glossary Page</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/preferences.html#algorithms-page" class="reference internal">4.2.12.5. Algorithms Page</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/connect.html" class="reference internal">4.2.13. Connect to a Memory on the Server</a>

</div>

</div>

<div id="the-glossary-windows" class="section">

<span id="gloss-windows"></span>

## 4.3. The Glossary Windows<a href="#the-glossary-windows" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

![Main Glossary Window](data:image/png;base64,)

Felix allows you to have any number of glossaries open. Glossaries can be as large as you like: the only limitation is your computer’s memory. You can open multiple glossaries in a single glossary window, or you can have multiple glossary windows.

<div class="admonition tip">

Tip

You can zoom the size of the text in the glossary window in and out. Click on the title bar of the window to make sure it’s active, then use Ctrl + Mouse Wheel to make the text larger or smaller.

</div>

**Sub-sections:**

<div class="toctree-wrapper compound">

- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/controlling.html" class="reference internal">4.3.1. Controlling the Glossary Windows</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/open.html" class="reference internal">4.3.2. Open a Glossary</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/matches.html" class="reference internal">4.3.3. Glossary Matches</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/edit-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.3.4. Edit an Entry</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/delete-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.3.5. Delete an Entry</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/add-entry.html" class="reference internal">4.3.6. Add an Entry to the Glossary</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/save.html" class="reference internal">4.3.7. Save the Glossary</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/manage.html" class="reference internal">4.3.8. Manage Your Glossaries</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/glosswindow/connect.html" class="reference internal">4.3.9. Connect to a Glossary on the Server</a>

</div>

</div>

<div id="edit-mode" class="section">

<span id="index-0"></span><span id="id1"></span>

## 4.4. Edit Mode<a href="#edit-mode" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

You can use Edit Mode to edit records displayed in the Memory and Glossary windows, and have those results reflected in the memory and glossary databases. Editing in the windows is very similar to editing in an ordinary word processing application, making it much easier to manage your memories and glossaries.

**Sub-sections:**

<div class="toctree-wrapper compound">

- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/enter-leave.html" class="reference internal">4.4.1. Entering and Leaving Edit Mode</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/enter-leave.html#enter-edit-mode" class="reference internal">4.4.1.1. Enter Edit Mode</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/enter-leave.html#leave-edit-mode" class="reference internal">4.4.1.2. Leave Edit Mode</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/edit.html" class="reference internal">4.4.2. Editing and Formatting Text</a>
- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/search.html" class="reference internal">4.4.3. Search and Replace</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/search.html#find-text" class="reference internal">4.4.3.1. Find Text</a>
  - <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/edit-mode/search.html#replace-text" class="reference internal">4.4.3.2. Replace Text</a>

</div>

</div>

<div id="search-replace" class="section">

<span id="index-1"></span><span id="id2"></span>

## 4.5. Search & Replace<a href="#search-replace" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

As of version 1.5, Felix has a separate window for searching and replacing translation units (TUs).

To do search and replace in Felix, press <span class="pre">`CTRL`</span>` `<span class="pre">`+`</span>` `<span class="pre">`F`</span> from the memory window to search/replace in your translation memories, and from the glossary window to search/replace in your glossaries. The Search window opens.

![The Felix Search window](data:image/png;base64,)

You can also use a menu selection to open the Search window. From the memory window, select *Edit ‣ Find ‣ Search Memory*. From the glossary window, select *Edit ‣ Find*.

The old search method is still available as the “Quick Search” command, directly below the “Find” menu items. See <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/memwindow/quick-search.html#quick-search" class="reference internal"><em>Quick Search</em></a> for details.

The window has a Search pane and a Replace pane:

- <a href="#search-pane" class="reference internal"><em>Search</em></a>: Search the TM or glossary
- <a href="#replace-pane" class="reference internal"><em>Replace</em></a>: Replace records in the TM or glossary.

Each translation record (entry) consists of several “fields”: the source text, the translation, context, the date the translation was created, and so on. By default, replacements are made for matching source and translation fields. You can also specify which field you want to perform the replacement in.

Enter a term to search for that term anywhere in a source or translation. Use tags in the format <span class="pre">`tag:term`</span> to make fine-grained searches. Below are the basic tags.

| Tag | Description |
|----|----|
| source: | Replace in the source field |
| trans: | Replace in the translation field |
| context: | Replace in the context field |
| created-by: | Replace the creator field |
| created: | Replace the date created |
| modified-by: | Replace the modified-by field |
| modified: | Replace the date modified |
| reliability: | Replace the reliability |
| validated: | Mark the record as validated (“true”) or not validated (“false”) |
| refcount: | Change the reference count of the record |

Click on the **Help** link on the Search and Replace panes for more tags.

<div id="examples" class="section">

### 4.5.1. Examples<a href="#examples" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

Replace ‘aaa’ in source and translation segments with ‘bbb’:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       aaa
    To         bbb

</div>

Replace ‘xxx’ in source segments with ‘yyy’:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       source:xxx
    To         yyy

</div>

Follow a tag by an asterisk (\*) to replace the entire field. For example, to change the “created-by” field of all matching records to “Ryan”:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       created-by:*
    To         Ryan

</div>

Change the record’s date created to 2009-10-01:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       created:
    To         2009-10-01

</div>

Make the reliability of the record 5:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       reliability:
    To         5

</div>

Validate the record:

<div class="highlight-python">

    From       validated:
    To         true

</div>

</div>

<div id="the-search-pane" class="section">

<span id="search-pane"></span>

### 4.5.2. The Search Pane<a href="#the-search-pane" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

To find translation units (TUs), enter a search term in the text box, and click Search. The matching TUs appear on the screen. You can keep adding more search terms to refine your search, edit or delete matches, or perform replace operations.

Searching works by using “filters.” Each term you enter in the search box is kept in a list of filters, which are shown on the right of the page. You can keep adding filters, or delete existing ones from the list if you are not getting enough matches.

Normally, the text you enter in the search box is matched against TU source, translation, and context values. If the term is found in one of these values, it’s a match.

You can also make more fine-grained searches using tags, or “commands.” For example, to find TUs with the string “giraffe” in the source, you would specify <span class="pre">`source:giraffe`</span>. You can find help on using search commands by clicking *Search Help \>\>* in the Search window.

</div>

<div id="index-2" class="section">

<span id="id3"></span>

### 4.5.3. Examples<a href="#index-2" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

The search feature supports regular expressions. To use a regular expression, use the “regex” tag as follows: <span class="pre">`regex:April`</span>` `<span class="pre">`\d{1,2},`</span>` `<span class="pre">`\d{4}`</span>

</div>

<div id="the-replace-pane" class="section">

<span id="replace-pane"></span>

### 4.5.4. The Replace Pane<a href="#the-replace-pane" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

To do a replace in your memory or glossary, in the Search window, click on the “Replace” link. You can perform the replace within just a subset of your memory/glossary, by first doing a search, and replacing within the search results. Simply perform a search, and then click the “Replace” link.

Enter the text you want to change in the “From” box, and the text you want to change it to in the “To” box. Normally, the replace is performed in the source, translation, and context fields of the TU. For example, if you specify to replace “hippo” with “zebra,” then any instance of “hippo” in a source, translation, or context field will be replaced by the word “zebra.”

You can also make more fine-grained replacements using tags, or “commands.” For example, to replace the word “car” with the word “skateboard,” but only if it’s in a source fields, specify “source:car” in the “From” box, and “skateboard” in the “To” box. You can find help on using replace commands here.

</div>

</div>

<div id="manage-your-memories" class="section">

<span id="mem-manage"></span><span id="index-3"></span>

## 4.6. Manage Your Memories<a href="#manage-your-memories" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

There is a new Memory Manager for Felix.

<div class="figure">

![The Memory Manger window](data:image/png;base64,)

The Memory Manager window

</div>

You can still use the old Memory Manager if you prefer it, or you are running an older version of Windows.

<div id="item-actions" class="section">

### 4.6.1. Item Actions<a href="#item-actions" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

In the row for each TM or glossary, there is a list of actions you can perform with that item.

**View**

> <div>
>
> View details about this TM/glossary.
>
> </div>

**Edit**

> <div>
>
> Edit details for this TM/glossary.
>
> </div>

**Browse**

> <div>
>
> Browse the translations in this TM/glossary.
>
> </div>

**QC**

> <div>
>
> Perform QC. See <a href="#quality-control" class="reference internal"><em>Quality Control (QC)</em></a> for details.
>
> </div>

**Remove**

> <div>
>
> Removes this TM/glossary. Felix will prompt you to save if you have made changes to the item.
>
> </div>

</div>

<div id="global-actions" class="section">

### 4.6.2. Global Actions<a href="#global-actions" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

At the bottom of the TM/glossary listing, there are also global actions you can perform.

**Load File**

> <div>
>
> Load a memory or glossary file.
>
> </div>

**Add New**

> <div>
>
> Add a new memory or glossary. It will appear at the top of the list, and contain no items.
>
> </div>

**Remove All**

> <div>
>
> Remove all translation memories or glossaries.
>
> </div>

</div>

<div id="changing-order-of-tms-glossaries" class="section">

### 4.6.3. Changing Order of TMs/Glossaries<a href="#changing-order-of-tms-glossaries" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

In the rightmost column of the TM and glosaary listings are up and down buttons. You can use this to move TMs and glossaries up and down in the list. New entries are added to the topmost translation memory or glossary.

</div>

<div id="quality-control-qc" class="section">

<span id="quality-control"></span><span id="index-4"></span>

### 4.6.4. Quality Control (QC)<a href="#quality-control-qc" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

You can perform quality control on your TMs and glossaries. Click **QC** in the row of the desired memory or glossary.

When you click **QC**, you are presented with a list of potential quality issues in the TM/glossary. The translation appears, and underneath it a message about the quality issue that was detected. A sample QC message is <span class="pre">`Number`</span>` `<span class="pre">`Check:`</span>` `<span class="pre">`Number`</span>` `<span class="pre">`3.2.11`</span>` `<span class="pre">`in`</span>` `<span class="pre">`source`</span>` `<span class="pre">`but`</span>` `<span class="pre">`not`</span>` `<span class="pre">`in`</span>` `<span class="pre">`target`</span>. You can choose to edit the translation, delete it, or view details about that translation.

To configure the QC settings, click **QC Settings** on the bottom of the Home view. The following QC settings are available:

**Numbers**  
Match numbers in source and target

**ALL CAPS**  
Match ALL CAPS words in source and target

**Glossary**  
Match glossary terms in source to translations in Target

**Live**  
Perform live checking. If you select this, then Felix will perform QC on each translation you add, as you are translating.

</div>

<div id="active-and-inactive-tms-glossaries" class="section">

### 4.6.5. Active and Inactive TMs/Glossaries<a href="#active-and-inactive-tms-glossaries" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

You can deactivate a TM or glossary. To activate or deactivate a TM/glossary, click **Edit** for that item, and clear the “Active” checkbox.

Deactivated memories and glossaries appear as light-gray text in the TM/glossary list. Although they can be used for concordance and searches, they are not used for TM and glossary searches.

</div>

<div id="old-memory-manager" class="section">

### 4.6.6. Old Memory Manager<a href="#old-memory-manager" class="headerlink" title="Permalink to this headline">¶</a>

You can have any number of memories open at one time — the only limit is the physical memory and disk space available on your computer. Use the Memory Manager dialog to manage your various memories.

To call up the Memory Manager dialog, from the **Tools** menu, select **Memory Manager…**

![Select Memory Manager from the Tools menu](data:image/png;base64,)

The Memory Manager dialog appears:

![The Memory Manager dialog](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Memory List  
The list of currently loaded memories. Memories whose checkboxes are selected are “active”; only active memories are included in searches and the like.

Move Up  
Move the selected memory up in the list. The top memory in the list is the “active” memory; new entries are added to this memory.

Move Down  
Move the selected memory down in the list. The top memory in the list is the “active” memory; new entries are added to this memory.

Add...  
Add (load) a memory into Felix.

Remove  
Remove (unload) a memory from Felix. This does not delete the memory from your hard disk.

Memory Info View  
Displays various information about the currently selected memory.

File Name:  
The file name of the currently selected memory.

Creator:  
The name of the user who created the memory.

Field:  
The field (subject) of the memory.

Created:  
The date that the memory was created.

Source Language:  
The source language of the memory.

Target Language:  
The target language of the memory.

Client:  
The client for whom the memory is used.

Num. Entries:  
The number of entries in the memory.

File Size:  
The size of the memory file on disk.

Reliability:  
The minimum, maximum, and average reliability of the memory’s entries.

Validated %:  
The percentage of the records that have been validated.

Locked:  
Whether the memory is locked. See below for details.

Edit Info  
Edit the memory information. When you click the button, the Information area becomes editable, and the button text changes to End Edit; you can change the values of the bold items. When you are done with your edits, click **End Edit**.

**OK**  
Accept your settings and close the dialog.

**Cancel**  
Discard your settings and quit the dialog.

You can also batch-set the reliability and validated flag, or lock/unlock a memory by clicking on **Advanced…**

![Advanced memory settings dialog](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Batch Set Reliability  
Set the reliability of all entries in the memory.

Batch Validate  
Validate/unvalidate every entry in the memory.

Locked  
Lock/unlock the memory. Only the creator of a memory can lock or unlock that memory. A locked memory can be searched, but it is not possible to add, edit, or delete the entries of a locked memory. If you wish to unlock a memory that you did not create, you must use the Save As... feature to save it as a different file. That will make you the creator of the new file, and allow you to unlock the memory.

This feature is useful if you have a reference memory (e.g. from a client) that you do not wish to/may not edit, or if you want to have a common translation memory to be referred to by multiple translators, but you do not want individual translators changing that memory.

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

- <a href="https://web.archive.org/web/20181022232000/http://felix-cat.com/media/manuals/felix/whats-new.html" class="reference internal">14. What’s New in Felix</a>

### Search

Enter search terms or a module, class or function name.
